channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Comelec reduces voters per precinct to prevent long lines in Bangsamoro elections
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) has implemented measures to ensure shorter voting lines and faster ballot processing during the Bangsamoro parliamentary elections on September 14. Comelec Chair ...

item.title: ACT-Wazalendo and CCM Commit to Political Reconciliation for Zanzibar's Development
item.description: The chairman of ACT-Wazalendo, Othman Masoud Othman, has assured the public that the ongoing political reconciliation process in Zanzibar is aimed at serving the broader interests of residents rather ...

item.title: Baruipur police encounter: Family rejects body, opposition condemns action
item.description: The last rites of Prabhas Mondal, one of the accused in the Baruipur rape and murder case, were conducted by local authorities after his family refused to claim his body. Mondal was killed in a police...

item.title: Bengaluru removes 430 km of footpath encroachments, to issue ID cards to street vendors
item.description: The Greater Bengaluru Authority (GBA) has cleared 430 km of footpath encroachments across the city between July 1 and 8, 2026, as part of its initiative to reclaim pedestrian spaces. The government as...

item.title: Ontario educators discuss key challenges in education policy
item.description: The article highlights an upcoming live discussion where Ontario teachers will address pressing educational concerns. Following student feedback on classroom issues like class sizes and special educat...

item.title: Kenya Lawyer Released After Arrest Over Stalled Vehicle, Alleged Political Motives
item.description: Lawyer Ndegwa Njiru, a key ally of DCP Leader Rigathi Gachagua, was released from police custody after being arrested following his vehicle's breakdown on Thika Highway. Njiru claims officers from Kas...

item.title: Yoruba-Hausa clash in Ibadan leaves four dead; Oyo State orders illegal structures removal
item.description: A violent clash between Yoruba traders and Hausa scavengers in Ibadan's Ojoo area left four people dead, prompting Oyo State to deploy security forces and order the removal of illegal structures. The ...

item.title: Two Candidates Tie for First Place in Real Estate Appraisers Licensure Exam
item.description: The Professional Regulation Commission (PRC) announced that two test takers tied for first place in the July 2026 licensure examination for real estate appraisers. Out of 1,456 examinees, 1,060 passed...

item.title: Soccer Players Modify Cleats to Alleviate Heel Discomfort
item.description: During the 2026 FIFA World Cup, Portuguese forward Pedro Neto's modified cleats sparked global attention, revealing a longstanding practice among athletes to address heel discomfort. The modification,...

item.title: Aviva Completes Full Ownership of Life Insurance JV in India
item.description: British insurance giant Aviva has finalized its acquisition of the remaining 26% stake in Aviva Life Insurance Company India from its joint venture partner Dabur Invest Corp, achieving full ownership....

item.title: Madlanga Commission Extends Deadline to Address Corruption in South Africa
item.description: The Madlanga Commission of Inquiry, tasked with investigating corruption in South Africa, has been granted an extended deadline to submit its final report. Originally due in March 2026, the report's s...

item.title: Strengthening El Nino Expected to Be One of the Strongest on Record: US Agency
item.description: The US Climate Prediction Centre (CPC) has warned that the El Nino weather pattern, which has been gaining strength over the past month, is highly likely to become one of the strongest on record when ...

item.title: Black Sabbath Drummer Bill Ward Embraces Wheelchair for Mobility, Stays Active at 78
item.description: Black Sabbath drummer Bill Ward, 78, has shared an update about his recent use of a wheelchair for mobility, reassuring fans that he remains active and healthy. In a social media post, Ward explained ...

item.title: Three Former TMC Rajya Sabha MPs Switch Allegiances to BJP Amid Factional Struggles
item.description: Three former Trinamool Congress (TMC) Rajya Sabha members—Sushmita Dev, Prakash Chik Baraik, and Sukhendu Sekhar Roy—have officially joined the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP), marking a significant shif...

item.title: Kenya Power to Conduct Scheduled Power Outages on July 10
item.description: Kenya Power has announced planned power outages scheduled for Friday, July 10, affecting various regions across the country. The national electricity distributor shared the notice through its official...

item.title: Iranians mourn Khamenei at his funeral amid regional tensions
item.description: The funeral of assassinated Iranian supreme leader Ali Khamenei in Mashhad has drawn massive crowds, with mourners demanding revenge against perceived aggressors. The burial, held in the Shrine of Ima...

item.title: Pogacar wins Tourmalet stage to take overall lead from Traeen
item.description: Tadej Pogacar secured a solo victory on the grueling Col du Tourmalet during the sixth stage of the 2026 Tour de France, reclaiming the overall lead from Torstein Traeen. The Slovenian rider's decisiv...

item.title: Criticism of Water Company CEO's Bonus Amid Environmental Concerns and Customer Complaints
item.description: Steve Crawford, a former surfing school operator in Scarborough, expressed outrage over Yorkshire Water's CEO Nicola Shaw receiving an additional £660,000 payment from parent company Kelda Holdings. C...

item.title: FIFA President Gianni Infantino Faces Scrutiny Over Red Card Decision and Political Ties
item.description: The article examines the controversy surrounding FIFA President Gianni Infantino's handling of a red card incident during a match, which sparked accusations of political bias. It highlights how Infant...

item.title: 6D Lotto Bettor Wins P8.7M Jackpot in PCSO Draw
item.description: A lone bettor secured the P8.7 million jackpot prize in the PCSO's 6D Lotto draw on July 9, 2026, by correctly guessing the winning numbers 1-8-6-4-9-4 in exact order. The odds of winning the jackpot ...
